# Mock IdP fixtures — SAML E2E (S5-1 Sprint 5 iter 2)

This directory contains a **test-only** SAML 2.0 Identity Provider fixture
that signs assertions against the SP (`SamlService`) under
`segurasist-api/src/modules/auth/saml/`.

## Why this exists

`cognito-local` (the local stack IdP emulator) does NOT implement SAML
federation — see NEW-FINDING-S5-1-01 in `docs/sprint5/feed/S5-1-iter1.md`.
The unit tests under `test/unit/modules/auth/saml/` already cover signature
verification and time bounds with an ephemeral keypair generated in
`beforeAll`. This directory ships the **persistable** fixture used by the
deferred E2E flow (`test/e2e/saml-flow.spec.ts`) and any local Playwright
run that wants to drive a SAML POST against the ACS endpoint without
spinning up a real Okta tenant.

## Files

- `keys/generate-keys.mjs` — idempotent RSA 2048 keypair generator. Creates
  `idp-private.pem` + `idp-public.pem` (and `.local.pem` mirrors).
- `keys/.gitignore` — keeps every `*.pem` out of git on purpose.
- `assertion-template.xml` — SAML assertion XML with placeholders
  (`{{tenantId}}`, `{{email}}`, `{{notOnOrAfter}}`, `{{nameId}}`,
  `{{issuer}}`, `{{notBefore}}`, `{{inResponseTo}}`, `{{role}}`).
- `sign-assertion.ts` — helper that takes a template + values + private
  key path and returns the **base64-encoded `<samlp:Response>`** ready to
  POST to `/v1/auth/saml/acs` as the `SAMLResponse` form field.

## SECURITY — read this before reusing the keypair

1. The keypair generated by `generate-keys.mjs` is for **test fixtures
   only**. It MUST NEVER be:
   - Configured as a tenant's `idpX509Cert` in any environment that holds
     real users (staging tenants tied to Cognito, prod, etc.).
   - Re-used as an SP signing key — the SP signs nothing in iter 1 (HTTP
     binding, unsigned AuthnRequest), and iter 2 will mint platform-level
     keys via KMS.
2. The script generates a fresh pair at first invocation; CI re-runs it on
   every workflow start so no artifact persists across pipelines.
3. If a developer accidentally publishes one of these PEMs, treat it as a
   non-incident **for prod** (no real tenant trusts it) but rotate the
   fixture set for hygiene by deleting the four files and re-running
   `generate-keys.mjs`.
4. The `.gitignore` adjacent to the keys covers the canonical names plus
   the `.local.pem` suffix that the dispatch rule allows.

## Usage from a test

```ts
import { ensureKeypair } from './keys/generate-keys.mjs';
import { signAssertion } from './sign-assertion';

beforeAll(() => ensureKeypair());

const samlResponseB64 = signAssertion({
  templatePath: 'test/fixtures/mock-idp/assertion-template.xml',
  values: {
    tenantId: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000aa',
    email: 'admin@example.test',
    role: 'admin_mac',
    notBefore: new Date(Date.now() - 60_000).toISOString(),
    notOnOrAfter: new Date(Date.now() + 5 * 60_000).toISOString(),
    inResponseTo: '_relay-fixture',
    issuer: 'https://idp.example.test/saml',
    nameId: 'admin@example.test',
  },
  privateKeyPath: 'test/fixtures/mock-idp/keys/idp-private.pem',
});
```

## Iter 3 / Sprint 6 follow-ups

- Replace `sign-assertion.ts` with a real Playwright fixture that runs
  against the **Okta dev tenant** (free up to 5k users, owned by SegurAsist).
- Generalize the template into a builder so encrypted assertions and
  multi-attribute statements can be composed declaratively.
- Wire the `SAML_TENANT_CONFIGS` env var to consume the public key from
  this fixture during E2E so the SP trusts the mock IdP for the duration
  of the run.
